perturbation.original_text: EFCA shall maintain the disclosing party’s Confidential Information in confidence and s hall exercis e in re lation thereto no lesser security measures and degree of care than t hose which the receiving party applies to its own confidential information and which EFCA warrants as providing adequate protection against unauthorised disclosur e, copying or use. EFCA shall ensure t hat disclos ure of such Confidential Information is restricted to those em ployees or direct ors of EFCA and EFCA’s principals having the need to know the same. Copi es or reproductions shall not be made exc ept to the ext ent reasonably necessary and all co pies made shall be the property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: EFCA shall maintain the disclosing party’s Confidential Information in confidence and s hall exercis e in re lation thereto no lesser security measures and degree of care than t hose which the receiving party deems necessary. EFCA shall ensure t hat disclos ure of such Confidential Information is restricted to those em ployees or direct ors of EFCA and EFCA’s principals having the need to know the same. Copi es or reproductions shall not be made exc ept to the ext ent reasonably necessary and all co pies made shall be the property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and which EFCA warrants as providing adequate protection against unauthorised disclosur e, copying or use' the responsibility of warranted security measures are being removed. This introduces in-text contradiction with clause 4.1, specifically 4.1.1 'not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and' which requires protection against disclosure. Since the warrant of providing adequate protection is being removed, the action of not divulging the information is therefore contradicted due to the vagueness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and
perturbation.justification: The original text included a warranty from EFCA that its security measures provide adequate protection against unauthorized disclosure, copying, or use. The changed text removes this warranty. The contradicted text (4.1.1) states that EFCA must 'not divulge the disclosing party’s Confidential Information'. By removing EFCA's warranted security measures, the changed text creates a contradiction. Without the warranted security measures, the obligation not to divulge the information becomes vague and potentially unenforceable because the level of security required is no longer guaranteed, directly conflicting with the obligation in clause 4.1.1, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  Return of Confidential Information 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  Return of Confidential Information 3.1 EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information, unless EFCA determines that retaining such information is necessary for its ongoing business operations, and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless EFCA determines that retaining such information is necessary for its ongoing business operations' contradicts the absolute obligation to return all confidential information. Clause 3.1.1 mandates immediate return, but the addition allows EFCA to retain information based on their own determination of business necessity. This in-text contradiction weakens the disclosing party's control over their confidential information and creates uncertainty about what information EFCA must actually return, while also contradicting the initial statement of sub-clause 3.1.1 about returning all documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 3.1.1 introduces an exception ('unless EFCA determines that retaining such information is necessary for its ongoing business operations') to the original absolute requirement to return 'all documents and materials'. This directly contradicts the original clause's mandate of returning 'all' confidential information immediately upon request, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly states that all documents must be returned, while the changed text allows EFCA to retain documents based on its own assessment, creating a direct conflict and weakening the disclosing party's rights, as per the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Limitations and Warrant y 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure. 4.2 The disc losing party reserves all ri ghts in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other t han those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenc e is hereby granted directly or ind irectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or ot her industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licen sable by the other party. Nothing herein contained shall be deemed to impose on either party any obligation to giv e the other party the opportunity of giving a quotation or making a tender to the ot her party or to enter into any contract for services or for supply of goods and/or materials with the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Limitations and Warrant y 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, unless such disclosure is in the best interest of EFCA; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure. 4.2 The disc losing party reserves all ri ghts in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other t han those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenc e is hereby granted directly or ind irectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or ot her industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licen sable by the other party. Nothing herein contained shall be deemed to impose on either party any obligation to giv e the other party the opportunity of giving a quotation or making a tender to the ot her party or to enter into any contract for services or for supply of goods and/or materials with the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless such disclosure is in the best interest of EFCA', it directly contradicts the initial statement 'not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party'. It creates an exception that allows EFCA to disclose confidential information based on their own subjective assessment of their 'best interest', undermining the confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.1.1 introduces an exception ('unless such disclosure is in the best interest of EFCA') to the original strict prohibition against divulging confidential information ('not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text by allowing disclosure under certain circumstances, thus creating a clear and direct conflict, and a strong in-text contradiction.
